steps.content: The equation $24h + 12c = 960$ models total earnings in dollars. Each term is (rate) × (hours), so each coefficient represents a dollar-per-hour rate.
The variable $h$ is defined as hours worked at the regular job. The number 24 is multiplied by $h$, so 24 is the hourly rate (dollars per hour) at the regular job.

Evaluate all choices:
  • A: "The amount, in dollars, the tutor earned for each hour she worked at her regular job" — 24 is the coefficient of $h$ (regular job hours), so this is the hourly rate for the regular job. That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
  • The amount, in dollars, the tutor earned for each hour she worked at her second job: Assigns 24 to the second job, but 24 multiplies $h$ (regular job), not $c$ (second job). Eliminated.
  • The number of hours the tutor worked in one week at her regular job: Interprets 24 as a count of hours, not a rate. Eliminated.
  • The number of hours the tutor worked in one week at her second job: Interprets 24 as hours at the second job; 24 doesn't multiply $c$. Eliminated.
